member.name: Robert Jenrick

addresses: YMCA, Lord Hawke Way, Newark, Nottinghamshire, NG24 4FH

member.synopsis: The Rt Hon Robert Jenrick is the Reform UK MP for Newark , and has been an MP continually since 5 June 2014.

officialSourceNotes: Current member identity, party, constituency, thumbnail, contact information, synopsis, written questions, and registered interests are generated from the official UK Parliament Members API.
Recent Commons division positions are generated from the official UK Parliament Commons Votes API.
External official links are retained as citations; TOR Phi profile and record navigation remains internal.

writtenQuestions.heading: Migrant Workers: Visas
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Business and Trade, whether he has made an estimate of the cost to his Department of subsidising visa costs for foreign national workers.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As part of the Industrial Strategy, the Chancellor launched the Visa Fees Reimbursement Scheme for Scale-Ups on 10 June. The targeted grant scheme, worth up to £25K for around 50 businesses a year, will help UK scale-ups in Clean Energy, Life Sciences and...

writtenQuestions.heading: Abu Zubaydah
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, on what specific date the Attorney General recused himself from discussions or decisions regarding the Government’s defence and settlement of Zubaydah v Foreign and Commonwealth Office; and whether he has had any communicatio
writtenQuestions.answerText: Without commenting on individual cases, the government’s robust conflict process would clearly preclude Law Officers having any involvement in cases in which they were instructed for other parties prior to appointment.By longstanding convention, recognise...

writtenQuestions.heading: Guantanamo Bay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether any settlement discussions have been initiated with representatives of Abu Faraj al-Libi, Mustafa al-Hawsawi, or Abd al-Rahim al-Nashiri.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Rt Hon Member knows, it has been the UK's longstanding position under successive governments, including the one he supported for over a decade, not to comment on intelligence related litigation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Guantanamo Bay
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, if she will publish a list of all current legal proceedings, including civil claims and complaints to the Investigatory Powers Tribunal, brought against her Department by cur
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Rt Hon Member knows, it has been the UK's longstanding position under successive governments, including the one he supported for over a decade, not to comment on intelligence related litigation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Abu Faraj al-Libi
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, whether her Department is defending a civil claim for damages brought by Abu Faraj al-Libi; and what estimate she has made of the potential liability to the public purse in r
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Rt Hon Member knows, it has been the UK's longstanding position under successive governments, including the one he supported for over a decade, not to comment on intelligence related litigation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Abu Zubaydah
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Foreign, Commonwealth and Development Affairs, what the total value is of the settlement agreed with Abu Zubaydah; and if she will provide a breakdown of (a) damages paid to the claimant and (b) legal costs paid to the cl
writtenQuestions.answerText: As the Rt Hon Member knows, it has been the UK's longstanding position under successive governments, including the one he supported for over a decade, not to comment on intelligence related litigation.

writtenQuestions.heading: Segregation of Prisoners: Civil Proceedings
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether there are any other live legal cases of prisoners challenging their detention in a Close Supervision Centre or Separation Centre.
writtenQuestions.answerText: I refer the right honourable Member to the answer I gave on 11 December to Question 97787.

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisons: Crimes of Violence
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many (a) murderers and (b) prisoners with a history of attacking prison officers are in open prisons for which the latest data is available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 30 September 2025, there were 266 prisoners with an index offence of murder in prisons whose predominant function is open in England and Wales.Information on the number of prisoners in the open estate with a history of attacking prison officers coul...

writtenQuestions.heading: HMP/YOI Chelmsford
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, on what dates he has officially visited HMP Chelmsford since his appointment; and whether he has met with the Governor to discuss the recent mistaken release.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Deputy Prime Minister met with the Governor of HMP Chelmsford on 24 October, the same day as the release in error of Hadush Kebatu. The Minister of State for Prisons, Probation and Reducing Reoffending is planning to visit HMP Chelmsford in the coming...

writtenQuestions.heading: HMP Wandsworth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, on what dates he has officially visited HMP Wandsworth since his appointment; and whether he has met with the Governor to discuss the recent mistaken release.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Minister of State for Prisons, Probation and Reducing Reoffending is planning to visit HMP Wandsworth in the coming weeks.

writtenQuestions.heading: Crown Prosecution Service: Remote Working
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Solicitor General, what the average daily office attendance rate was for Crown Prosecution Service staff in the most recent quarter for which figures are available.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Crown Prosecution Service does not maintain a central record of office attendance rates. Office attendance is monitored at a local level.

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoners: Transgender People
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many transgender prisoners with convictions for (a) sexual and (b) violent offences are housed within the general female prison estate; and how many of those prisoners have been granted a Ministerial exemptio
writtenQuestions.answerText: As of 1 December, there were no transgender women, including individuals with Gender Recognition Certificates, in the general women's prison estate with any convictions for sexual offences and five or fewer transgender women with convictions for violent o...

writtenQuestions.heading: Segregation of Prisoners
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether any offenders currently detained in (a) separation and (b) close supervision centres are challenging their detention.
writtenQuestions.answerText: There are no ongoing judicial review challenges made with regards to separation centres and no ongoing challenges that have reached the courts with regards to close supervision centres.

writtenQuestions.heading: Independent Review of Separation Centres
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, for what reasons Jonathan Hall KC’s review into Separation Centres has not yet been published; and when he plans to publish that review.
writtenQuestions.answerText: The Government is carefully considering the recommendations made by Jonathan Hall KC and will publish his report in due course.

writtenQuestions.heading: Juries
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, whether the proposed removal of the right to trial by jury for offences carrying a maximum sentence of less than three years will apply retrospectively to defendants who have already entered a plea of not guilty
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers will introduce detailed proposals to Parliament as soon as Parliamentary time allows.

writtenQuestions.heading: HMP Wandsworth
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, when he plans to visit HMP Wandsworth.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Ministers regularly visit prisons across the country. Any plans to visit specific prisons will be notified to the relevant Members of Parliament in advance.

writtenQuestions.heading: Juries: Crown Court
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how much his Department has spent on the construction of new Crown Court courtrooms with full jury facilities in the last year.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the last full financial year (2024/25), the Department has spent £3,131,757 on property projects that include creating new jury enabled Crown courtrooms. Some of these courtrooms are flexible use and can be used for multiple purposes. This figure inclu...

writtenQuestions.heading: Homicide and Rape: Crown Court
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, what proportion of Crown court cases are for murder, manslaughter and rape.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the 12 months to June, there were 114,325 cases disposed of at the Crown Court for all offences, 3,615 for rape, 483 for murder and 49 for manslaughter – rape accounts for 3.0%, and murder and manslaughter combined make up less than 0.1% of all disposa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Homicide and Rape: Crown Court
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many murder, manslaughter and rape cases were dealt with in the Crown court in the past 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: In the 12 months to June, there were 114,325 cases disposed of at the Crown Court for all offences, 3,615 for rape, 483 for murder and 49 for manslaughter – rape accounts for 3.0%, and murder and manslaughter combined make up less than 0.1% of all disposa...

writtenQuestions.heading: Prisoner Escapes
writtenQuestions.questionText: To ask the Secretary of State for Justice, how many prisoners have absconded from or failed to return to open prisons in the past 12 months.
writtenQuestions.answerText: Open prisons play an important role in preparing individuals for safe resettlement into the community, and Release on Temporary Licence (ROTL) is a key part of that process. We have robust risk assessments and monitoring in place to minimise absconds and ...
